regions.name: Australia
regions.blurb: Tax return season for the 2025–26 income year opens 1 July 2026. People who lodge themselves have until 31 October; clients on a registered agent's lodgment program get considerably longer.

rows.title: Tax return season opens
rows.note: Lodge from 1 July; ATO pre-fill data flows in through July.

rows.title: Self-lodgment deadline
rows.note: Individual returns if you lodge yourself — and the cut-off to appoint an agent for the extension.

rows.title: Tax-agent program deadline
rows.note: Extended due date for most returns lodged via a registered tax agent.

regions.name: India
regions.blurb: The e-filing portal is open for AY 2026-27. Salaried individuals (non-audit) file by 31 July; business and audit cases run later into the year.

rows.title: Belated & revised returns
rows.note: Last window before ITR-U; late fee + interest apply.

regions.name: UAE
regions.period: Corporate Tax · Federal Decree-Law No. 47 of 2022
regions.blurb: There is no single season — your corporate tax return and payment are due 9 months after your own financial year-end. Use the calculator to find your exact date.
facts: 9% on taxable income above AED 375,000; 0% on the first AED 375,000.
Even 0%-rated free-zone persons and nil filers must register and file.
The AED 10,000 late-registration penalty is waived if the first return is filed within 7 months of the first tax period's end.
Late filing: AED 500/month (first 12 months), then AED 1,000/month; late payment 14% per annum.
